mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0%, restrictive policy reduces the present value of earnings and increases recession risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% have average 20-day S&P 500 forward returns that are not below the unconditional average by at least 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% signals inflation pressure under already restrictive policy, forcing markets to price tighter real financial conditions and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between 3-month CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS above 4.0% has a non-negative coefficient for 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor demand and future earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the average triggered return exceeds the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous 3-month increases in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicate resilient household income and consumption demand, supporting near-term revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises over 3 months and PAYEMS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS and PAYEMS positive-growth signal does not produce a higher mean 20-day S&P 500 return than either single-series signal in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and operating leverage for listed firms, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends have a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing of HOUST below 1200 indicates weakening housing-related demand and tighter credit-sensitive activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings below 1200 are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in PAYEMS growth combined with a rising UNRATE signals a labor-market inflection from expansion to contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth slows and UNRATE rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS slowdown and UNRATE rise interaction has non-negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or no lower downside hit rate than unconditional returns.
